articles.author: Aisha Malik
articles.title: Tim Cook is stepping down as CEO of Apple. Here's a look at his 15-year legacy, from new products and services to China expansion. - TechCrunch
articles.description: Cook, who joined Apple in 1998, succeeded Steve Jobs as CEO in 2011 and went on to transform Apple into a $4 trillion powerhouse.
articles.content: After 15 years at the helm, Tim Cook is stepping down as CEO of Apple and handing over the reins to the companys senior vice president of hardware engineering, John Ternus. Cook, who joined Apple in … [+5542 chars]

articles.title: Framework's CEO on the RAM crisis and creating a "MacBook Pro for Linux users" - Ars Technica
articles.description: We actually have slightly more Linux users than Windows users."
